author: @tehspamgozehere
text:
We have something very similar to that that's made out of big cast iron plates that link together on lugs and is then cemented with a bit of fire cement around the cracks. The damn thing looks like an Easter Island monolith. The front door hinges downward and is on heavy duty springed runners so it can slide in under the bottom of the unit out of the way, allowing a grating to be put in the front instead to get a semi-open fire. The door latch is a big sliding lump in the top center of the fold-down door. The door takes up the lower half of the front... So when it's almost closed, the door looks like the front of a face, the round intake hole looks like a mouth, the door latch looks like a nose and the light from the fire escapes above and beside the nose to look like two ominously glowing eyes. And if you get it really going hot, it glows a dull ruddy red.

We affectionately call it Mergatroid, and I love it.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Yeah, I guess efficient is oversating the case a little - but certainly cool

author: @Bertoaus
text: Simple... but efficient? I don't know.
